title: Implement StuckTaskEscalator service
context: Tasks can get stuck in_progress indefinitely. Need a new service that detects stuck tasks (2h threshold) and creates inbox alerts, with optional auto-reset at 4h. See design: docs/stuck-task-escalation-design.md. The existing monitor_enhanced.check_stuck_tasks() skips tasks with active WorkflowRuns — this new service needs to catch BOTH tasks with no run (Case A) and tasks with a stale running workflow run (Case B).
acceptance: StuckTaskEscalator.check(db) queries in_progress tasks older than flag_hours (default 2h)
Skips tasks where task.agent in ['reflection','sweep'] OR title starts with [reflection],[sweep],[internal],[diagnostic] (case-insensitive)
Case A: in_progress task with no active WorkflowRun → creates InboxItem after flag_hours
Case B: in_progress task with WorkflowRun in status=running but run.updated_at > flag_hours ago → creates InboxItem
Dedup: does not create second InboxItem if one with id matching stuck_{task_id}_ exists with modified_at within last flag_hours*2 hours
InboxItem id format: stuck_{task.id}_{unix_timestamp}; title: '⏰ Task stuck in_progress for {Xh}: {title[:60]}'

Auto-reset: if reset_hours > 0 and task has been stuck > reset_hours AND a prior alert exists, set work_state=not_started, failure_reason='Auto-reset: stuck in_progress for {N}h', cancel active WorkflowRuns (status=cancelled)
Config: reads flag_hours/reset_hours/skip_projects from OrchestratorSetting; defaults to 2/4/[]
check() returns dict: {flagged: int, reset: int, skipped: int}
All exceptions caught and logged — method never raises
Unit tests cover: filter logic, dedup, threshold edge cases (just under/just over), both Case A and B, auto-reset, config override

title: Wire StuckTaskEscalator into engine monitor loop
context: After the core service (task 1) is implemented, wire it into the engine. The engine monitor loop calls monitor_enhanced.run_full_check() on each cycle — call stuck_task_escalator.check(db) right after. See docs/stuck-task-escalation-design.md and app/orchestrator/engine.py lines around 649-656.
acceptance: StuckTaskEscalator instantiated once (at engine init or first monitor call)
Called after monitor_enhanced.run_full_check() in the monitor cycle
Result logged: '[ENGINE] Stuck escalation: flagged=N, reset=N'
Wrapped in try/except so errors never crash the engine loop
No regression: existing monitor behavior unchanged
